Today is February 10, 2026. Regarding the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, what specific action did the FBI observe the masked individual performing in the newly recovered surveillance footage?
(A) Picking up vegetation from the ground to cover the camera lens
(B) Using a specialized tool to disconnect the doorbell's power supply
(C) Spraying a dark substance directly onto the lens of the Nest camera
(D) Removing the recording device from the door frame entirely
(E) Placing a piece of adhesive tape over the motion sensor
explanation: According to the article, the FBI released videos showing the person approach the front door, check the camera, and then pick up "some vegetation from the ground and using it to cover the camera lens."
model_output:
In the investigation into the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, the mother of *Today* show co-host Savannah Guthrie, authorities faced initial hurdles because her Nest doorbell camera had been disconnected and she did not have a cloud subscription. However, on February 10, 2026, the FBI released newly recovered surveillance footage retrieved from residual data in Google's backend systems. 

The chilling video showed an armed, masked individual approaching Guthrie's front door. In the footage, the suspect is seen holding a flashlight in their mouth and grabbing vegetation (which law enforcement described as prairie brush or plants) from the yard, using it to cover the camera lens in an attempt to conceal their identity and tamper with the device. 

This specific action matches option (A).

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- PBS ([https://www.pbs.org](https://www.pbs.org))
- CBS News ([https://www.cbsnews.com](https://www.cbsnews.com))
- NBC News / YouTube ([https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nbcnews](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nbcnews))

Today is February 10, 2026. During the congressional hearing regarding the deaths of Alex Pretti and Renee Good, how many Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents were confirmed to have already received their body-worn cameras?
(A) 6,000
(B) 3,000
(C) 10,000
(D) 13,000
(E) 20,000
explanation: Todd Lyons, the acting head of ICE, stated during the hearing that "more than 3,000 ICE officers have received their cameras."
model_output:
During the House Homeland Security Committee hearing on February 10, 2026, which addressed the deaths of Alex Pretti and Renee Good, Acting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) Director Todd Lyons was questioned by Representative Bennie Thompson regarding the deployment of body-worn cameras [1, 2]. 

In his testimony, Lyons confirmed that approximately **3,000** ICE agents currently had active body-worn cameras in the field out of a total of about 13,000 agents [2]. He also noted that an additional 6,000 cameras were in the process of being deployed [2]. (During the same hearing, Customs and Border Protection Commissioner Rodney Scott stated that about 10,000 out of 20,000 Border Patrol agents had received their cameras [2].)

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Forbes: ICE And CBP Heads Refuse Questions On Alex Pretti And Renee Good's Deaths In Heated Congressional Hearing] (https://www.forbes.com)
- [YouTube / KARE 11: 'How many cameras...on your agents,' Congress presses Trump immigration officials after shootings] (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qTeHCJEcJ435UsTCO8vw3Qd78wv3Tnxe)

Today is February 10, 2026. According to the FBI summary of the 2019 interview with Michael Reiter, what specific description did Donald Trump allegedly use for Ghislaine Maxwell during a 2006 phone call?
(A) A disgusting creep
(B) A professional recruiter
(C) A dangerous associate
(D) An evil operative
(E) A disgusting operative
explanation: The document claims Reiter said Trump told him Ghislaine Maxwell was Epstein's "operative" and that "she is evil."
model_output:
Based on the recently released FBI documents summarizing a 2019 interview with former Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter, Donald Trump allegedly called Reiter in July 2006 regarding the Jeffrey Epstein investigation. 

During the phone call, Trump reportedly expressed support for the police probe, referred to Epstein as "disgusting," and specifically pointed authorities toward Ghislaine Maxwell. According to Reiter's account to the FBI, Trump described Maxwell as Epstein's **"evil operative"** (stating she was his "operative" and that she was "evil"), urging the police to focus their investigation on her. 

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [TIME: Trump Reportedly Told Police 20 Years Ago That 'Everyone' Knew About Epstein] (https://time.com)
- [Sportskeeda: "Nice self own": Harry Sisson calls Trump "pedo protector" as reports of him contacting police about Epstein & Ghislaine Maxwell in 2006 go viral] (https://www.sportskeeda.com)

Today is February 10, 2026. What is the estimated cost of the Gordie Howe International Bridge project as mentioned in the report?
(A) $3.4bn CAD
(B) $6.4bn CAD
(C) $10.2bn CAD
(D) $2.6bn CAD
(E) $8.1bn CAD
explanation: The article states: "It is estimated to have cost $6.4bn CAD (£3.4bn)."
model_output:
To determine the estimated cost of the Gordie Howe International Bridge project, we can look at the official figures released by the Windsor-Detroit Bridge Authority and the project's construction consortium, Bridging North America. 

The Gordie Howe International Bridge is a massive infrastructure project connecting Detroit, Michigan, and Windsor, Ontario. According to recent reports and project updates, the comprehensive contract cost—which covers the design, construction, financing, and 30 years of operations and maintenance—is estimated at **$6.4 billion CAD**. The project is entirely funded by the Canadian government, which plans to recoup the investment through toll revenues. 

Therefore, the correct option is (B).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

(E) 15

model_output:
Following their review of the unredacted Epstein files at the Department of Justice on February 10, 2026, Congressmen Thomas Massie and Ro Khanna identified a specific document containing a list of **20** individuals. 

The lawmakers pointed out that in the previously released version of this file—which included names, birthdays, and photographs (resembling a photo lineup)—almost every individual's name had been completely redacted except for Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell. Following public pressure from the congressmen, the Justice Department subsequently removed the redactions for 16 additional names on that 20-person list. 

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

(C) October 31, 2025
(D) October 26, 2025
(E) September 26, 2025
explanation: The article states: "Their journey began on 26 October 2025 at the Huong Dao Vipassana Bhavana Center in Fort Worth."
model_output:
To find the date when the Buddhist monks began their 2,000-mile "Walk of Peace," we can look at the details of their journey. 

The "Walk for Peace" was a long-distance pilgrimage initiated by Buddhist monks from the Hương Đạo Vipassana Bhavana Center in Fort Worth, Texas, to Washington, D.C., to promote peace, compassion, and nonviolence. The group of monks, accompanied by a rescue dog named Aloka, officially began their 2,300-mile trek from Fort Worth on **October 26, 2025**. They walked for 108 days, eventually arriving in Washington, D.C., on February 10, 2026.

Therefore, the correct date is October 26, 2025.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

- The Texas Tribune (https://www.texastribune.org)

According to reports (including coverage by the BBC), the hit hockey romance series was created and produced in Canada on a relatively minimal budget compared to other major television shows. The reported production budget was **less than C$5 million per episode** [2]. 

*(Note: Articles often list the budget as "C$5m ($3.6m; £2.6m) per episode," making C$5m the correct Canadian dollar figure, while $3.6m and £2.6m represent the US Dollar and British Pound equivalents, respectively [2].)*

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 10, 2026. In what year was the ban on indigenous potlatch gatherings officially lifted in Canada?
(A) 1970
(B) 1914
(C) 1800
(D) 2015
(E) 1951
explanation: The article states: "The ban was lifted in 1951, and in 2015 Canada's Truth and Reconciliation Commission found that it was part of a 'cultural genocide'..."
model_output:
The ban on Indigenous potlatch gatherings in Canada was officially lifted in **1951**. 

The Canadian government originally banned the potlatch—a vital cultural, political, and economic ceremony for many Indigenous peoples of the Pacific Northwest Coast—in 1884 (becoming law in 1885) through an amendment to the Indian Act. The government viewed the practice as a barrier to the assimilation of Indigenous peoples into Euro-Canadian society. For 67 years, the ceremony was driven underground, and those caught participating faced confiscation of their sacred ceremonial items and imprisonment. 

The ban remained in effect until 1951, when the Indian Act was heavily revised and the section outlawing the potlatch and other traditional ceremonies was finally deleted.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- The Canadian Encyclopedia: Potlatch Ban (https://www.thecanadianencyclopedia.ca/en/article/potlatch-ban)
- Wikipedia: Potlatch ban (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Potlatch_ban)

(A) From four days a week to five days a week
(B) From five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day
(C) From 40 hours a week to 60 hours a week
(D) From eight hours a day to ten hours a day
(E) From five days a week to seven days a week
explanation: Lyons stated the schedule had been accelerated "from five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day."
model_output:
To answer your question, according to testimony given by Acting ICE Director Todd Lyons to the House Homeland Security Committee on February 10, 2026, the ICE training schedule was accelerated by increasing both the number of days per week and the hours per day. 

Lyons stated: *"The meat of the training was never removed, [but] the timeline was we took training from five days a week, eight hours a day, to six days a week, 12 hours a day"*.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 10, 2026. Which specific consumer category saw a 0.9% month-over-month decline in sales at the end of 2025?
(A) Clothing retailers
(B) Electronics
(C) Furniture stores
(D) Gasoline
(E) Building materials
explanation: The article states: "Furniture store sales fell 0.9% month-over-month, while sales at clothing retailers declined 0.7%."
model_output:
To determine which consumer category saw a 0.9% month-over-month decline at the end of 2025, we can look at the U.S. retail sales data for December 2025 (which was widely reported in early 2026). 

According to the Commerce Department's retail sales report for December 2025:
*   **Furniture stores** and miscellaneous store retailers both experienced a **0.9% decline** in sales.
*   Clothing retailers saw a 0.7% decline.
*   Electronics and appliance stores saw a 0.4% decline.
*   Gasoline stations actually saw a 0.3% increase.
*   Building material and garden equipment dealers posted a 1.2% increase.

Therefore, furniture stores are the specific category from your options that recorded a 0.9% drop.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Trading Economics (https://tradingeconomics.com/)
- Bitget (https://www.bitget.com/)

(A) 2006
(B) 1990
(C) 2008
(D) 2004
(E) 2019
explanation: The article states: "the president and the White House have repeatedly said he was in the dark about Epstein's crimes before he broke off contact with him in around 2004..."
model_output:
According to statements made by President Donald Trump, he broke off contact with Jeffrey Epstein in 2004. In July 2019, following Epstein's arrest on federal sex trafficking charges, Trump told reporters that he had a falling out with Epstein and had not spoken to him in 15 years, which places the end of their relationship in 2004. Various reports also point to a 2004 real estate bidding war over a Palm Beach mansion as the catalyst for their rift. 

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 10, 2026. What is the name of the proposed legislation aimed at removing the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse to file civil claims?
(A) Giuffre's Act
(B) The Epstein Transparency Act
(C) The Survivors Justice Bill
(D) The Roberts Law
(E) Virginia's Law
explanation: The article mentions survivors gathered to announce "Virginia's Law, legislation that aims to remove the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse to file civil claims."
model_output:
The proposed legislation aimed at removing the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse and trafficking to file civil claims is **Virginia's Law**. 

Introduced on February 10, 2026, by Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer and Representative Teresa Leger Fernández, the bill is named in honor of the late Virginia Giuffre, one of the most prominent survivors and accusers of the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Under previous federal law, adult survivors generally had to bring civil claims within 10 years of the abuse. "Virginia's Law" seeks to eliminate this statute of limitations entirely, allowing survivors to pursue justice and hold their abusers (and those who enabled them) financially accountable regardless of how much time has passed. 

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [CBS News] (https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/chuck-schumer-virginias-law-statute-of-limitations-sex-abuse-trafficking/)
- [Congresswoman Teresa Leger Fernández - House.gov] (https://fernandez.house.gov/media/press-releases/leger-fernandez-schumer-introduce-virginias-law-eliminate-statute-limitations)

Today is February 10, 2026. For how long has Ilia Malinin remained unbeaten in figure skating competition?
(A) Almost two and a half years
(B) Since the 2022 Olympics
(C) Exactly three years
(D) Five years
(E) One year
explanation: The article states: "He has not been beaten in competition for almost two and a half years."
model_output:
To determine how long Ilia Malinin has remained unbeaten as of February 10, 2026, we can look at his competition history. 

Ilia Malinin's last defeat in an individual figure skating competition occurred at the **ISU Grand Prix de France**, which took place from **November 3–5, 2023**. At that event, he finished in second place behind France's Adam Siao Him Fa. 

Following that loss, Malinin embarked on a historic winning streak, capturing gold at 14 consecutive individual competitions. This dominant run included winning the Grand Prix Final, U.S. National Championships, and World Championships multiple times. 

Calculating the time from his last loss in early November 2023 to the current date of February 10, 2026, exactly 2 years and 3 months (or 2.25 years) have passed. Therefore, he has remained unbeaten for almost two and a half years. 

*(Note: His individual unbeaten streak would later come to a shocking end just a few days after this date, when he missed the podium at the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics men's free skate on February 13, 2026.)*

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Reddit: Ilia Malinin has won his 14th consecutive gold medal... (https://www.reddit.com/r/FigureSkating/comments/18h5z1z/ilia_malinin_has_won_his_14th_consecutive_gold/)

To determine the leader of the band of Buddhist monks completing the "Walk of Peace," we can look at the details of their 2,300-mile journey from Fort Worth, Texas, to Washington, D.C., which concluded in February 2026. 

The pilgrimage was organized by monks from the Hương Đạo Vipassana Bhavana Center (which eliminates options B and C, as these are parts of the center's name). <final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>
